session_summary: Fixed test_starvation_detection Test 3 counting logic bug - full test suite now passes with zero failures

details: Test used `grep | wc -l | grep -q '[2-9]'` to check for >= 2 starvation reports
Regex [2-9] matches any string containing digit 2-9, but fails for '10', '11', '20', etc.
Test generates ~10 starvation reports (3-12 seconds), count '10' failed regex check
This was NOT a buffering or I/O issue - data was always in the file
investigation: User correctly identified that log_msg() writes to stderr (unbuffered)
Confirmed redirection `> file 2>&1` properly captures both stdout and stderr
Found the actual bug: counting logic failed for double-digit counts
solution: Added count to success/failure messages for debugging
Removed unnecessary sleep that was workaround attempt
bug_fix.commit: 84d5a5ac13b6 - tests: Fix test_starvation_detection Test 3 flawed counting logic

notes: All timing fixes from previous sessions remain stable
test_starvation_detection now passes on both backends
test_starvation_threshold passes on both backends (kernel worker filter working)

commits_this_session.message: tests: Fix test_starvation_detection Test 3 flawed counting logic
commits_this_session.impact: Resolves false failure when test generates 10+ starvation reports

resolved: test_starvation_threshold kernel worker interference (fixed with -i filter)
Output buffering timing issues (stop_stalld before checking logs)
remaining: queue_track backend limitation: Cannot detect SCHED_FIFO tasks on runqueue (BPF task_running() check only tracks __state == TASK_RUNNING)
This affects FIFO-related tests but is a known architectural limitation

key_technical_insights.stderr_handling: stalld log_msg() writes to stderr when verbose (-v flag), which is unbuffered by design
key_technical_insights.redirection: Test redirection `> file 2>&1` correctly captures both stdout and stderr
key_technical_insights.regex_pitfalls: Character class [2-9] matches any string CONTAINING those digits, not comparing numeric values
key_technical_insights.proper_counting: Use `grep -c` for counts, then numeric comparison with -ge/-gt/-eq
key_technical_insights.debugging_output: Always include actual values in test failure messages for easier diagnosis

test_coverage_summary.phase_2_cli_options: 9/10 tests passing (test_force_fifo skipped by user request)

environment.stalld_build: Rebuilt on RHEL-10 to avoid GLIBC mismatch

next_steps: Consider full matrix testing (backends × threading modes)
Consider stress testing and edge cases
Update test documentation with lessons learned
lessons_learned: Regex character classes vs numeric comparison: [2-9] is not '>= 2'
Always test with realistic data volumes (10+ reports exposed the bug)
Include actual values in test output for debugging
Investigate user hypotheses first - stderr unbuffering was a good lead
Root cause: Logic bugs can masquerade as timing/I/O issues
